skill.name: testing-strategy
skill.description: Plan and implement a practical testing strategy with Vitest, React Testing Library, and Playwright.
skill.summary: Build unit, integration, and end-to-end test coverage with clear targets and workflow guidance.
skill.author: AI-Vibe-Prompts

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, URL, environment, weak-crypto, and heuristic patterns. Review found these are Markdown documentation examples for testing setup, local Playwright configuration, and sample tests, with no autonomous execution or credential exfiltration. The skill is low risk because users may copy package installation and test runner commands.

low_findings.title: Documentation Includes Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are documentation examples for npm, npx, test scripts, CI steps, and opening local reports. They are not executed by the skill, but users should review package and CI commands before copying them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside fenced Markdown examples and package script snippets. No file contains runnable automation that invokes them without user action.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is Limited To CI Examples
low_findings.description: The environment variable detections reference process.env.CI in a Playwright configuration example. This controls retries, workers, and server reuse and does not read secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment variable shown is CI, a standard non-secret flag. No evidence found of credential access or environment file reading.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Local Or Test Targets
low_findings.description: The network detections are localhost Playwright base URLs and a test API URL used in a mocking example. No evidence found of data exfiltration or calls to suspicious external endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are used for local E2E configuration and mocked test setup. The skill does not perform network requests.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and critical combination alerts do not match the reviewed content. The referenced lines are frontmatter, diagrams, imports, test names, or examples, not cryptographic or reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic functions, reconnaissance instructions, obfuscation, or malicious combination. The alerts appear caused by generic token matching in Markdown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copyable Package Installation And Test Runner Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides commands that users may paste into a terminal. This is expected for a testing setup guide, but commands can install dependencies and run browser tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and copyable. The risk is low because they are documentation examples, not hidden execution paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: CI Workflow Example Runs Third-Party Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub Actions example uses checkout, setup-node, Codecov, and artifact upload actions. These are common testing workflow examples, but users should pin action versions according to their policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow snippet is explicit and easy to review. It is not executed by the skill, but third-party CI actions are a real operational consideration.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss key test layers or add slow tests without clear purpose. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical unit, integration, and E2E test planning.
actual_capabilities: Explains a testing pyramid with unit, integration, and E2E distribution.
Provides Vitest configuration guidance with coverage thresholds.
Provides Playwright configuration guidance for browser E2E tests.
Shows test examples for components, API mocking, forms, and async behavior.
Outlines CI test jobs and package script patterns.
Recommends coverage targets for critical paths and business logic.
limitations: Focused on JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Vitest, and Playwright projects.
Does not inspect a repository or generate project-specific tests by itself.
Command examples require user review before installation or CI use.
Coverage targets are general guidance and may need adjustment.

use_cases.title: Set Up Tests For A New Frontend
use_cases.description: Create a balanced first test plan with Vitest, React Testing Library, and Playwright.

use_cases.title: Raise Coverage On Existing Features
use_cases.description: Identify which unit, integration, and E2E tests should cover important behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize CI Test Gates
use_cases.description: Define test scripts and CI workflow steps for pull requests and releases.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Basic Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple test plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-strategy skill to create a basic unit, integration, and E2E test plan for my React feature.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Vitest
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate setup for unit and integration testing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-strategy skill to recommend a Vitest setup with coverage targets for this TypeScript React project.

prompt_templates.title: Design E2E Flows
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-strategy skill to choose the highest-value Playwright E2E flows for authentication, checkout, and account settings.

prompt_templates.title: Build CI Quality Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-strategy skill to design a CI testing workflow with fast pull request checks and full pre-release validation.

output_examples.input: Create a test strategy for a new dashboard feature.
output_examples.output: A layered plan that prioritizes unit tests for calculations, integration tests for data loading, and E2E tests for the critical dashboard flow.

output_examples.input: Improve coverage for our login page.
output_examples.output: A focused recommendation covering validation, failed login states, successful redirects, and one browser-level authentication flow.
A staged workflow with linting, type checks, unit coverage, build validation, and Playwright runs before deployment.

best_practices: Use many fast unit tests and reserve E2E tests for critical user journeys.
Mock external dependencies in lower-level tests to keep results stable.
Run fast checks before commits and full suites before releases.
anti_patterns: Do not use E2E tests for every small component behavior.
Do not chase coverage numbers while ignoring business-critical paths.
Do not copy CI or install commands without reviewing project constraints.

faq.question: Which tools does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Vitest, React Testing Library, and Playwright for modern JavaScript and TypeScript projects.

faq.question: Can it create tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides strategy, examples, and patterns. A coding agent still needs project context to write exact tests.

faq.question: What coverage target does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends about 80 percent minimum coverage, with higher coverage for critical paths and business logic.

faq.question: Is it only for React projects?
faq.answer: Most examples use React, but the testing pyramid and workflow guidance apply more broadly.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. It includes local and test URL examples, but the skill itself does not make network requests.

faq.question: Should I run every command shown?
faq.answer: No. Review commands against your package manager, security policy, and CI environment first.
